You were not created randomly. You were created intentionally for a purpose, and that purpose is God’s glory.

This changes how a man sees his life. Work is not just work. Leadership is not just responsibility. Family is not just relationships. Everything becomes an opportunity to reflect God.

Many men struggle with purpose because they look inward. They try to define themselves based on what they want, feel, or achieve.

But purpose is not found within. It is found in God.

When a man understands that he was created for God’s glory, everything gains meaning.

Reflection

Do I see my life as something for me or something for God?

Challenge

View one part of your daily life today as an opportunity to glorify God.

Prayer

Creator God, help me live out the purpose You designed for me.

---

study_lessons.title: God’s Glory in Creation
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 19:1
“The heavens are telling of the glory of God; And the expanse is declaring the work of His hands.”

Study

Creation exists to display God’s glory. The universe is not silent. It is constantly declaring who God is.

From the vastness of the stars to the detail of life, everything points to His greatness.

Men often overlook this because they are distracted. They move quickly, focus on tasks, and miss what is right in front of them.

Creation is not just something to enjoy. It is something to learn from. It reveals God’s power, creativity, and order.

A man who slows down and pays attention will see God’s glory everywhere.

Reflection

Do I notice God’s glory in the world around me?

Challenge

Spend time observing creation today and reflect on God’s greatness.

Prayer

God, open my eyes to see Your glory in everything You have made.

---

study_lessons.title: God’s Glory in Salvation
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Ephesians 1:6
“To the praise of the glory of His grace…”

Study

Salvation is not ultimately about us. It is about God’s glory.

God saves sinners to display His grace. Every life changed by the gospel becomes evidence of His power and mercy.

Men often see salvation only as personal benefit. Forgiveness, peace, and eternal life. Those are real, but they are not the main point.

The main point is that God is glorified through redemption.

Your story is not just about you. It is about what God has done in you.

A man who understands this will live differently. He will not hide his faith. He will not minimize what God has done. He will live as a testimony.

Reflection

Do I see my salvation as something personal or something that brings glory to God?

Challenge

Share your testimony with someone this week.

Prayer

God, use my life to display Your grace.

---

study_lessons.title: Living for God’s Glory
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

1 Corinthians 10:31
“Whether, then, you eat or drink or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.”

Study

Every part of life is meant to glorify God. Not just church, not just spiritual moments, but everything.

This means how you work matters. How you lead matters. How you speak matters. How you handle pressure matters.

There is no divide between sacred and secular. All of life belongs to God.

Men often compartmentalize their faith. They live one way at church and another way everywhere else. But Scripture calls for consistency.

Glorifying God means representing Him well in every area.

A man who lives for God’s glory lives with purpose and integrity.

Reflection

Are there areas of my life that are not aligned with God’s glory?

Challenge

Choose one area of life to intentionally honor God today.

Prayer

Lord, help me live every part of my life for Your glory.

---

study_lessons.title: Pride vs God’s Glory
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Proverbs 16:18
“Pride goes before destruction, And a haughty spirit before stumbling.”

Study

The greatest enemy of God’s glory in a man’s life is pride.

Pride shifts focus from God to self. It seeks recognition, control, and credit. It resists correction and avoids humility.

God’s glory and human pride cannot coexist. One will replace the other.

Men often fall into pride without realizing it. Success leads to self-reliance. Achievement leads to self-focus.

But pride always leads to destruction. It weakens leadership, damages relationships, and pulls men away from God.

Humility restores alignment. It brings focus back to God where it belongs.

Reflection

Where is pride showing up in my life?

Challenge

Practice humility in one specific way today.

Prayer

God, remove pride from my heart and refocus me on Your glory.

---

study_lessons.title: A Life That Reflects His Glory
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Matthew 5:16
“Let your light shine before men in such a way that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.”

Study

God’s glory is not meant to stay hidden. It is meant to be seen through the lives of His people.

Jesus calls men to live in a way that points others to God. Not for personal recognition, but for God’s glory.

This means living with integrity, courage, and consistency. It means standing for truth and reflecting Christ in everyday life.

A man who lives this way becomes a light. Not because he is perfect, but because he is aligned with God.

The goal is not to draw attention to yourself. The goal is to point people to God.

A life lived for God’s glory leaves an impact that lasts.

Reflection

Does my life point people toward God or toward myself?

Challenge

Do something today that reflects God’s character to others.

Prayer

God, let my life reflect Your glory in everything I do.

study_lessons.title: The Reality of God
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Genesis 1:1
“In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.”

Study

The Bible does not begin by arguing for God’s existence. It declares it. “In the beginning God…” Before anything existed, God was already there. He is not part of creation. He is the Creator.

Modern culture often treats belief in God as optional or outdated. But Scripture presents God as the starting point of everything. Life, purpose, morality, and truth all flow from Him.

Every man builds his life on a foundation. Some build on success, others on money, identity, or personal achievement. But if the foundation is not God, it will not hold.

The existence of God is not just a theological concept. It is the most important reality a man must face. If God is real, then He has authority. If He has authority, then we are accountable.

Strong men do not ignore this question. They settle it.

Reflection

Do I live with daily awareness that God is real and present?

Challenge

Read Genesis chapters 1 and 2 today.

Prayer

God, help me live with a clear awareness of Your reality and authority.

---

study_lessons.title: Creation Declares God
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 19:1
“The heavens are telling of the glory of God;
And the expanse is declaring the work of His hands.”

Study

Creation is constantly pointing to God. The complexity of the universe, the order of nature, and the design of life all declare that there is a Creator.

Men often overlook what is right in front of them. They see the world but miss the message. Creation is not random. It is evidence of intelligence, power, and purpose.

Psalm 19 says the heavens are telling. That means creation is actively speaking. Every sunrise, every star, every detail of life points beyond itself to God.

You do not need a theology degree to see that design requires a designer. Order requires a source. Life requires a giver.

A man who pays attention to creation will begin to see the fingerprints of God everywhere.

Reflection

When I look at creation, do I see randomness or design?

Challenge

Spend time outside today and reflect on God as Creator.

Prayer

Creator God, open my eyes to see Your power and design in the world around me.

---

study_lessons.title: The Inner Witness
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Romans 2:15
“…in that they demonstrate the work of the Law written in their hearts, their conscience bearing witness…”

Study

God has not only revealed Himself outwardly through creation. He has also revealed Himself inwardly through conscience. Every man has an internal sense of right and wrong.

This moral awareness is not accidental. It is evidence that we are made in the image of God. The conscience points to a moral Lawgiver.

Even those who deny God still live as if moral truth exists. They speak of justice, fairness, and right and wrong. But those ideas only make sense if there is an ultimate standard.

The inner voice that convicts, warns, and guides is not random. It is part of God’s design. It reminds us that we are accountable and that truth exists beyond personal opinion.

A man who listens to his conscience will begin to recognize his need for God.

Reflection

Do I listen to my conscience or ignore it?

Challenge

Pay attention today to moments when your conscience speaks.

Prayer

God, sharpen my awareness of right and wrong and draw me closer to You.

---

study_lessons.title: Faith Is Not Blind
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Hebrews 11:1
“Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.”

Study

Faith is often misunderstood as blind belief. But biblical faith is not irrational. It is grounded in truth, evidence, and revelation.

Hebrews describes faith as assurance and conviction. It is confidence based on what God has revealed. Faith is not believing without reason. It is trusting what is true even when it cannot be seen physically.

Men are called to think, examine, and understand. Christianity does not ask men to shut off their minds. It calls them to use their minds rightly.

Creation, conscience, and Scripture all point to God. Faith responds to that evidence with trust and obedience.

A strong man does not fear questions. He seeks truth and stands on it.

Reflection

Do I view faith as weak belief or strong conviction?

Challenge

Write down why you believe God is real.

Prayer

Lord, strengthen my faith and deepen my conviction in Your truth.

---

study_lessons.title: The Fool Denies God
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 14:1
“The fool says in his heart, ‘There is no God.’”

Study

The Bible speaks directly about denying God. It does not describe it as intellectual strength, but as foolishness. This is not an insult. It is a diagnosis.

To deny God is to ignore the evidence of creation, the witness of conscience, and the truth of Scripture. It is to live as if there is no authority above oneself.

Many men reject God not because of lack of evidence, but because of resistance to authority. If God is real, then He has the right to rule. That challenges human independence.

Calling it foolish highlights the seriousness of rejecting truth. It leads to confusion, broken morality, and unstable living.

A wise man humbles himself before truth. He does not resist it.

Reflection

Where am I tempted to live as if God is not in control?

Challenge

Identify one area where you need to submit to God’s authority.

Prayer

God, remove pride from my heart and help me walk in truth.

---

study_lessons.title: Seeking God
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Jeremiah 29:13
“And you will seek Me and find Me when you search for Me with all your heart.”

Study

God is not hiding from those who genuinely seek Him. He promises that those who pursue Him with sincerity will find Him.

Seeking God requires effort. It is not passive. It involves time in Scripture, prayer, reflection, and humility. Men who seek God casually rarely grow. Men who pursue Him intentionally find clarity and direction.

God responds to pursuit. He draws near to those who desire Him.

This is not about perfection. It is about sincerity. A man who honestly seeks God will encounter Him.

Reflection

Am I pursuing God intentionally or casually?

Challenge

Set aside focused time today to seek God through prayer and Scripture.

Prayer

Lord, give me a heart that truly seeks You.

---

study_lessons.title: Living in Light of God’s Reality
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Hebrews 11:6
“And without faith it is impossible to please Him, for he who comes to God must believe that He is and that He is a rewarder of those who seek Him.”

Study

Believing that God exists is the starting point. But it must lead to action. Hebrews says we must believe that He is and that He rewards those who seek Him.

Faith is not just acknowledgment. It is alignment. It shapes how a man lives, leads, and makes decisions.

If God is real, then every part of life matters. Work matters. Leadership matters. Integrity matters. Every decision is made in the presence of God.

Men who live with awareness of God’s reality walk differently. They lead differently. They endure differently.

The question is not just, “Do I believe God exists?” The question is, “Am I living like He does?”

Reflection

Does my life reflect belief in a real and present God?

Challenge

Make one decision today based on God’s authority, not convenience.

Prayer

God, help me live every day in light of Your reality.

study_lessons.title: God Is Holy
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Isaiah 6:3
“And one called out to another and said, ‘Holy, Holy, Holy, is Yahweh of hosts, The whole earth is full of His glory.’”

Study

The holiness of God is one of the most important truths in Scripture. To say God is holy means He is completely set apart from sin. He is pure, perfect, and without flaw.

Isaiah’s vision shows that heaven is centered on God’s holiness. The angels do not cry out “love” or “power” three times. They cry out “holy.” This is central to who God is.

Many men have a casual view of God. They think of Him as relaxed, easygoing, or tolerant of sin. But the Bible presents a God who is completely pure and completely righteous.

When Isaiah saw God’s holiness, his response was immediate conviction. He recognized his own sin. This is what happens when men see God clearly. It changes how they see themselves.

God’s holiness should produce both reverence and humility. It reminds us that sin is serious and that God is not to be taken lightly.

Reflection

Do I view God with reverence, or have I become too casual in my thinking?

Challenge

Spend time today reflecting on Isaiah chapter 6.

Prayer

Holy God, help me see You as You truly are and respond with reverence.

study_lessons.title: God Is Just
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Deuteronomy 32:4
“The Rock! His work is perfect, For all His ways are just; A God of faithfulness and without injustice, Righteous and upright is He.”

Study

God is perfectly just. That means He always does what is right. He never acts unfairly. He never overlooks sin. He never makes mistakes.

In a world filled with injustice, this truth matters. Human systems fail. Courts fail. Leaders fail. But God never fails in justice.

Many people struggle with the idea of God’s justice because it means accountability. If God is just, then sin must be addressed. Wrong must be judged.

God’s justice is not separate from His goodness. It is part of it. A good God must be just. If He ignored sin, He would not be good.

For men, this means our lives matter. Our actions matter. We are accountable to a righteous Judge.

Reflection

Do I take God’s justice seriously in how I live?

Challenge

Examine one area of your life that needs correction.

Prayer

God, help me live with awareness that You are just and righteous.

study_lessons.title: God Is Righteous
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 11:7
“For Yahweh is righteous, He loves righteousness; The upright will behold His face.”

Study

God is not only just in what He does. He is righteous in who He is. Righteousness means that everything about God is morally perfect.

God does not follow a standard of righteousness. He is the standard.

This means that what is right is defined by God’s character, not by human opinion. Culture does not determine morality. God does.

Men often struggle with this because it removes personal authority. It means we do not get to define right and wrong for ourselves.

But this is also freeing. It means truth is not constantly changing. It is fixed in the character of God.

A man who aligns his life with God’s righteousness will walk in clarity and integrity.

Reflection

Do I define right and wrong based on God’s Word or my own opinion?

Challenge

Identify one area where you need to align with God’s standard.

Prayer

Righteous God, shape my life according to Your standard.

study_lessons.title: God Is Perfect
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Matthew 5:48
“Therefore you are to be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect.”

Study

God is perfect in every way. There is no flaw, weakness, or imperfection in Him. His character, His decisions, and His actions are always complete and without error.

Jesus calls believers to reflect this perfection. Not in a way that earns salvation, but in a way that pursues holiness and maturity.

Men often settle for “good enough.” But God does not operate that way. His perfection sets the standard for growth and pursuit.

Understanding God’s perfection should challenge complacency. It should push men toward growth, discipline, and maturity.

God does not call men to passivity. He calls them to pursue Christlikeness.

Reflection

Where have I settled for “good enough” instead of pursuing growth?

Challenge

Choose one area to intentionally grow in this week.

Prayer

Lord, help me pursue growth and not settle for mediocrity.

study_lessons.title: God Is Unchanging
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Malachi 3:6
“For I, Yahweh, do not change…”

Study

God does not change. His character, His truth, and His promises remain constant. In a world that constantly shifts, this is a stabilizing truth.

Everything around us changes. Culture changes. Opinions change. Circumstances change. But God remains the same.

This means His Word is reliable. His promises are trustworthy. His character is consistent.

Men need stability. Without it, life becomes uncertain. But when a man builds his life on an unchanging God, he gains confidence and security.

You can trust a God who never changes.

Reflection

Do I trust God as stable, or do I allow circumstances to shake my faith?

Challenge

Write down one promise of God you need to trust.

Prayer

Unchanging God, help me stand firm in Your truth.

study_lessons.title: God Is Sovereign
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 115:3
“But our God is in the heavens; He does whatever He pleases.”

Study

God is sovereign. That means He has complete authority and control over all things. Nothing happens outside of His knowledge or permission.

This can be difficult to understand, but it is essential to believe. God is not reacting to events. He is ruling over them.

Men often try to control everything. They want certainty, security, and outcomes. But sovereignty reminds us that God is in control, not us.

This truth produces peace. It removes anxiety. It builds trust. When a man understands God’s sovereignty, he can face uncertainty with confidence.

God is not overwhelmed. He is in control.

Reflection

Do I trust God’s control, or do I try to control everything myself?

Challenge

Surrender one worry to God today.

Prayer

Sovereign God, help me trust Your control over my life.

study_lessons.title: Responding to Who God Is
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Romans 11:33
“Oh, the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God!”

Study

When we begin to understand who God is, the only right response is worship, humility, and obedience.

The attributes of God are not just ideas to study. They are truths that should shape how we live.

God is holy, so we pursue holiness.
God is just, so we live with integrity.
God is righteous, so we align with truth.
God is perfect, so we pursue growth.
God is unchanging, so we trust Him.
God is sovereign, so we surrender control.

The more clearly a man sees God, the more clearly he will live.

The goal of theology is not information. It is transformation.

Reflection

How should my life change based on what I now understand about God?

Challenge

Write down one way you will live differently this week.

Prayer

God, shape my life according to who You are.

study_lessons.title: Approach the Word with Purpose
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Ezra 7:10
“For Ezra had set his heart to study the law of Yahweh and to practice it, and to teach His statutes and ordinances in Israel.”

Study

Strong men do not drift into spiritual growth. They pursue it with intention. Ezra did not casually read the Word. He set his heart to study it. That means he made a decision ahead of time that Scripture would be a priority in his life.

Many men struggle with Bible reading because they approach it without purpose. They read randomly, inconsistently, and without direction. Over time, that leads to frustration and inconsistency.

Ezra gives a clear pattern: study, practice, and teach. He learned the Word, lived the Word, and then passed it on. This is the rhythm of biblical manhood.

Studying Scripture requires discipline. It means setting aside time, removing distractions, and focusing your attention. It means coming to the Word ready to learn, not just to check a box.

A man who approaches Scripture with purpose will grow. A man who treats it casually will drift.

Reflection

Do I approach God’s Word intentionally or casually?

Challenge

Set a specific time and place for Bible study this week.

Prayer

Lord, give me discipline to pursue Your Word with purpose.

---

study_lessons.title: Observation: See What the Text Says
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Nehemiah 8:8
“They read from the book, from the law of God, translating to give the sense so that they understood the reading.”

Study

The first step in studying Scripture is observation. Before you try to interpret or apply a passage, you must see what it actually says.

Observation requires slowing down. It means reading carefully, paying attention to details, and asking basic questions. Who is speaking? Who is being addressed? What is happening? What words are repeated? What stands out?

Many men skip this step. They jump straight to what the passage means to them. But if you do not first understand what the text says, you will misunderstand what it means.

Observation trains your mind to pay attention. It builds discipline and focus. It forces you to engage Scripture instead of skimming it.

God’s Word rewards careful reading. The more you observe, the more you will see.

Reflection

Do I rush through Scripture, or do I slow down and pay attention?

Challenge

Read a short passage today and write down 3 observations.

Prayer

God, help me see clearly what Your Word is saying.

---

study_lessons.title: Interpretation: Understand the Meaning
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

2 Timothy 2:15
“Be diligent to present yourself approved to God as a workman who does not need to be ashamed, accurately handling the word of truth.”

Study

Once you observe the text, the next step is interpretation. This is where you ask, “What does this passage mean?”

Interpretation requires effort. Paul tells Timothy to be diligent and to handle the Word accurately. That means we must take Scripture seriously. We cannot twist it to fit our opinions or use it out of context.

To interpret correctly, consider the context. What comes before and after the passage? What is the main point? How would the original audience have understood it?

Scripture interprets Scripture. Clear passages help explain more difficult ones. The goal is not to make the Bible say what we want. The goal is to understand what God actually said.

Men who handle Scripture carelessly become confused. Men who handle it accurately grow in clarity and confidence.

Reflection

Do I try to understand Scripture correctly, or do I read my own ideas into it?

Challenge

Take one verse today and write what it actually means in context.

Prayer

Lord, help me handle Your Word with accuracy and respect.

---

study_lessons.title: Application: Live What You Learn
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

James 1:22
“But become doers of the word, and not merely hearers who delude themselves.”

Study

Bible study is incomplete without application. Knowledge alone is not the goal. Obedience is. James warns that hearing the Word without doing it leads to deception.

Application answers the question, “What should I do because of this truth?”

This is where Scripture moves from information to transformation. God’s Word is meant to change how you think, speak, lead, and live.

Many men enjoy learning but resist applying. They gain knowledge but avoid change. But real growth happens when truth is put into practice.

Application requires humility. It requires honesty. It requires action. A man who applies Scripture will grow. A man who only studies it will stall.

Reflection

What is one truth I have learned but not applied?

Challenge

Apply one specific truth from today’s reading.

Prayer

God, give me the courage to live out what I learn.

---

study_lessons.title: Consistency Builds Strength
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 1:2
“But his delight is in the law of Yahweh, And in His law he meditates day and night.”

Study

Spiritual strength is built over time. It does not come from occasional bursts of effort. It comes from consistent habits.

Psalm 1 describes a man who meditates on God’s Word day and night. This is not about perfection. It is about consistency. He returns to Scripture regularly. He builds a rhythm of engagement.

Many men start strong but struggle to stay consistent. Life gets busy. Distractions increase. Discipline fades. But growth requires persistence.

Consistency compounds. Small daily effort leads to long-term strength. Over time, the man who stays in the Word becomes rooted, steady, and wise.

A strong life is built through steady habits.

Reflection

Am I consistent in Scripture, or do I go through cycles?

Challenge

Commit to reading Scripture every day this week.

Prayer

Lord, build consistency in my life and discipline in my habits.

---

study_lessons.title: Remove Distractions
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Luke 10:41–42
“But the Lord answered and said to her, ‘Martha, Martha, you are worried and bothered about so many things; but only one thing is necessary…’”

Study

Distraction is one of the greatest obstacles to spiritual growth. In a world filled with noise, notifications, and constant activity, focus is becoming rare.

Jesus told Martha that she was distracted by many things, but only one thing was necessary. The same is true for men today. Many things compete for attention, but time in God’s Word is essential.

Studying Scripture requires intentional focus. It means putting the phone down, turning off distractions, and giving God your full attention.

Men who never slow down will never go deep. Depth requires focus. Growth requires intentional time.

If you want to grow, you must remove what distracts you.

Reflection

What distracts me most when I try to spend time with God?

Challenge

Turn off all distractions during your Bible study today.

Prayer

God, help me remove distractions and focus fully on You.

---

study_lessons.title: Build a Lifetime Habit
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Joshua 1:8
“This book of the law shall not depart from your mouth, but you shall meditate on it day and night… so that you may be careful to do according to all that is written in it.”

Study

Bible study is not a short-term project. It is a lifelong pursuit. God told Joshua to stay in His Word continually. The goal was not just knowledge, but obedience and success in living rightly.

Men who build a habit of studying Scripture create a foundation that lasts. Over time, the Word shapes their thinking, strengthens their convictions, and directs their lives.

This is not about perfection. It is about commitment. A man who stays in the Word over years will become grounded, wise, and steady.

The goal is not to finish a study. The goal is to become a man who lives in the Word.

Reflection

What would my life look like if I stayed consistent in Scripture for years?

Challenge

Commit to continuing this 52-week journey.

Prayer

Lord, help me build a lifelong habit of studying Your Word.

study_lessons.title: Why Revelation Matters
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Deuteronomy 29:29

“The secret things belong to Yahweh our God, but the things revealed belong to us and to our sons forever, that we may observe all the words of this law.”

Study

Human beings naturally ask questions about God. Who is He? What is He like? Why are we here? What does He expect from us? These questions rise in every human heart because we were created by God and designed to know Him.

But without revelation, those questions would remain unanswered. People could speculate about God endlessly, but speculation never produces certainty. Philosophers form theories. Cultures build religions. Individuals create personal ideas about God. Yet none of those efforts can produce true knowledge of Him.

That is why revelation is necessary.

Revelation simply means that God makes Himself known. Instead of leaving humanity to guess about His nature, God has spoken and acted in ways that allow us to know Him truthfully.

Deuteronomy reminds us that some things remain hidden in the wisdom of God. There are mysteries beyond our understanding. Yet God has revealed what we need to know in order to live faithfully before Him. He has not revealed everything, but He has revealed enough.

The tragedy today is not that God has remained silent. The tragedy is that people often ignore what He has already revealed. Men search for spiritual experiences while neglecting Scripture. They chase opinions about God while overlooking the truth God has already spoken.

Strong faith begins with humility. It begins with recognizing that knowing God is possible only because He has chosen to reveal Himself.

Reflection

Do I approach God’s revealed truth with humility and seriousness?

Challenge

Read Psalm 19 today and notice how creation and Scripture both reveal God.

Prayer

Lord, thank You for revealing Yourself. Help me take seriously the truth You have made known.

---

study_lessons.title: General Revelation Through Creation
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 19:1

“The heavens are telling of the glory of God; And the expanse is declaring the work of His hands.”

Study

God reveals Himself to all people through creation. This is called general revelation because it is available to everyone, everywhere.

When we look at the world around us, we see evidence of God’s power, wisdom, and creativity. The order of the universe, the complexity of life, and the beauty of creation all testify that there is a Creator.

Psalm 19 describes the heavens as constantly declaring God’s glory. The sky does not use words, but its message is clear. Every sunrise, every mountain range, and every star-filled night quietly announces that the world did not create itself.

General revelation tells us that God exists and that He is powerful. It shows us that the universe is not random and that life has design and purpose.

However, many people ignore this testimony. Instead of recognizing the Creator, they explain away creation or attribute it to chance. Scripture teaches that this rejection is not because the evidence is weak but because the human heart often resists acknowledging God.

For men who are willing to see it, creation constantly points beyond itself. The world is not the ultimate reality. It is a sign pointing to the One who made it.

Every time we look at creation, we are being reminded that there is a Creator who deserves our attention and our worship.

Reflection

When I see creation, does it lead me to think about the Creator?

Challenge

Spend a few minutes outside today noticing the details of creation and thanking God for His work.

Prayer

Creator God, open my eyes to see Your glory displayed in the world around me.

---

study_lessons.title: General Revelation Through Conscience
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Romans 2:15

“…in that they demonstrate the work of the Law written in their hearts, their conscience bearing witness and their thoughts alternately accusing or else defending them.”

Study

God also reveals Himself through the human conscience. Every person has an inner sense that recognizes right and wrong. This moral awareness is another form of general revelation.

Even people who have never read the Bible often know that certain actions are wrong. They feel guilt when they lie, steal, or harm others. That sense of moral accountability points to the reality that a moral law exists.

And if there is a moral law, there must also be a moral Lawgiver.

Romans 2 explains that the work of God’s law is written on human hearts. Conscience acts like an internal witness, reminding people that their actions are being measured against a moral standard.

However, conscience is not perfect. Over time it can become distorted or hardened. Repeated sin can dull a person’s sense of right and wrong. Culture can also influence how people interpret moral feelings.

Even so, conscience still testifies that humans were created to live under God’s authority. It reminds us that our actions matter and that we are accountable for how we live.

General revelation through conscience helps explain why every culture wrestles with questions of justice, fairness, and morality. Deep down, people know that right and wrong are real.

Reflection

How does my conscience respond when I ignore what I know is right?

Challenge

Pay attention today when your conscience alerts you to something that needs to change.

Prayer

Lord, keep my conscience sensitive to truth and quick to respond to Your conviction.

---

study_lessons.title: The Limits of General Revelation
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Romans 1:21

“For even though they knew God, they did not glorify Him as God or give thanks, but they became futile in their thoughts, and their foolish heart was darkened.”

Study

General revelation tells us that God exists, but it does not reveal everything we need to know about Him. Creation shows His power and glory, and conscience points to His moral authority. But neither of these reveals the full story of salvation.

Creation cannot explain the cross. Nature does not teach the details of forgiveness, grace, or redemption. The stars declare God’s greatness, but they do not explain how sinners can be reconciled to Him.

Because of this limitation, general revelation leaves humanity responsible but not redeemed.

Romans 1 teaches that people often respond to general revelation by suppressing the truth. Instead of honoring God, they ignore Him. Instead of giving thanks, they pursue their own desires.

This is why humanity needs something more than general revelation. We need God to speak more clearly and more specifically. We need Him to explain who He is, what He has done, and how we can be saved.

General revelation points toward God. Special revelation explains Him.

Reflection

Do I recognize that knowing about God is different from knowing Him personally?

Challenge

Thank God today for revealing more than just His existence.

Prayer

God, thank You for revealing truth that goes beyond what creation alone can show.

---

study_lessons.title: Special Revelation Through Scripture
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

2 Timothy 3:16

“All Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, for training in righteousness.”

Study

Special revelation is God’s clearer and more detailed communication with humanity. While general revelation is seen in creation and conscience, special revelation comes through God’s spoken and written Word.

Scripture is the primary form of this revelation.

The Bible is not merely a religious book written by human thinkers. It is described as God-breathed, meaning its ultimate source is God Himself. Through the work of the Holy Spirit, God guided human authors to record His truth accurately.

Because Scripture is God’s Word, it carries authority. It teaches us what is true, corrects us when we are wrong, and trains us to live in righteousness.

Without Scripture, we would know very little about God’s character, His promises, or His plan of redemption. The Bible reveals the story of creation, the fall of humanity, God’s covenant promises, the coming of Christ, and the hope of eternal life.

Special revelation is God’s gift to humanity. It provides clarity where human understanding fails.

Reflection

Do I treat Scripture as the voice of God speaking truth into my life?

Challenge

Read one chapter of the Bible slowly today, paying attention to what it reveals about God.

Prayer

Lord, give me a deeper respect for Your Word and a stronger desire to learn from it.

---

study_lessons.title: The Authority of Scripture
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Psalm 119:160

“The sum of Your word is truth, And every one of Your righteous judgments is everlasting.”

Study

Because Scripture comes from God, it carries absolute authority. It is not simply advice or inspiration. It is truth that stands above every human opinion.

Cultures change. Philosophies shift. Popular beliefs rise and fall. But God’s Word remains constant. It does not adjust to cultural trends or personal preferences.

The authority of Scripture means that God’s Word has the final say in matters of faith, truth, and morality. It corrects our thinking when we drift from truth. It challenges our behavior when we compromise. It provides guidance when we face uncertainty.

For many people, the idea of authority is uncomfortable. They prefer to decide truth for themselves. But rejecting God’s authority does not produce freedom. It produces confusion.

Strong men anchor their lives in the authority of Scripture. They allow God’s Word to shape their beliefs, direct their decisions, and define their priorities.

A life built on God’s Word stands firm when everything else shifts.

Reflection

Do I allow Scripture to challenge my thinking, or only affirm what I already believe?

Challenge

Identify one area of life where you need to submit more fully to biblical truth.

Prayer

Father, help me trust the authority of Your Word above every other voice.

---

study_lessons.title: Building Life on God’s Word
study_lessons.content:
Scripture (LSB)

Matthew 7:24

“Therefore everyone who hears these words of Mine and does them may be compared to a wise man who built his house on the rock.”

Study

Jesus taught that the stability of a person’s life depends on the foundation they build upon. A house built on rock can endure storms. A house built on sand collapses under pressure.

The same principle applies to spiritual life.

Men who build their lives on opinions, emotions, or cultural values eventually discover how unstable those foundations are. When trials come, those beliefs cannot sustain them.

But men who build their lives on God’s Word develop a firm foundation.

Hearing Scripture is important, but Jesus emphasizes something deeper. The wise man both hears and obeys. Truth becomes the foundation of his decisions, priorities, and actions.

This is the ultimate purpose of revelation. God has not revealed truth merely to inform us but to transform us. His Word is meant to shape how we live.

When a man builds his life on Scripture, he becomes steady. His convictions deepen. His leadership strengthens. His life begins to reflect the wisdom of God.

Revelation is not just information about God. It is the foundation for a faithful life.

Reflection

What foundation am I building my life on right now?

Challenge

Commit to finishing this 52-week journey and building your life on God’s Word.

Prayer

Lord, help me build my life on the solid foundation of Your truth.

podcasts.title: Episode 115: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #115- Ed Uszynski and Brian Smith
podcasts.description:
Seventy percent of kids quit sports before age 13. Despite youth sports being a $40 billion industry, the more parents spend, the less kids enjoy it. The “youth sports industrial complex” is failing to deliver on its promises, and Christian fathers in particular have a crucial role to play in changing that story.That’s why veteran sports ministers Brian Smith and Ed Usyznski wrote Away Game: A Christian Parent’s Guide to Navigating Youth Sports. With over 50 combined years in sports ministry, multiple theological degrees, and decades discipling athletes at every level, they are the most trusted voices speaking biblically into this issue today.Instead of treating youth sports as performance-driven pressure cookers, Away Game reframes them as opportunities for discipleship. Every practice, game, and car ride home becomes a chance to connect sports emotions, joy, disappointment, competition, teamwork, to spiritual formation.

This book equips men and fathers to:


Lead their homes with a biblical vision for sports.
Leverage athletics as a training ground for humility, gratitude, and resilience.
Turn the car ride home into discipleship instead of conflict.
Resist toxic sports culture by modeling kingdom values.
Brian and Ed are available for interviews on themes like:


Why “sports builds character” is a myth, and how to redeem it.
Mastering the car ride home as a father.
Why Christian men must model “play over performance” in an achievement-driven culture.
How to disciple kids through both winning and losing.

podcasts.title: Episode 114: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #114- Frank Sells
podcasts.description: Frank Sell is the founder of Get Shit Done Coffee and living proof that the American Dream is still alive. He grew up hearing the usual advice: get good grades, go to college, get a job. But he knew early on that path wasn’t for him. School didn’t make sense, so he learned a trade. With nothing but $100, a VHS tape, and a drive towards freedom, Frank quit his job and hit the pavement, learning a new trade. As he puts it, he beat the streets so hard he swears he’ll get a bill for the potholes! No mentors, no handouts, just raw hustle. Today, that same drive powers Get $h!t Done Coffee, built for those who want more out of life and are willing to work for it. Frank’s journey is simple but powerful. If he can do it, so can you. All you need is heart, grit, and the will to GET $h!t DONE!  https://homeofthehustle.com/

podcasts.title: Episode 113: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #113- Standing Firm in Dark Times
podcasts.description:
Standing in Dark Times
 In this powerful sermon, Pastor Jody reminds us that following Jesus was never promised to be easy. We live in a world filled with fear, compromise, and uncertainty, yet God has called His people to stand firm in the midst of darkness. Drawing from the truth of Scripture, Pastor Jody shows how the light of Christ shines brightest when the night seems the darkest.

This message will encourage you to anchor yourself in God’s Word, lean on the power of the Holy Spirit, and live boldly for Christ even when the culture pushes against you. If you’ve ever felt weary, discouraged, or pressured to give up, this sermon will strengthen your faith and remind you that God has not left you powerless.

Listen in and be challenged to rise up, stand strong, and walk faithfully no matter what trials you face.

podcasts.title: Episode 112: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #112- Congressman Barry Loudermilk
podcasts.description:
Barry Loudermilk is a Constitutional Conservative who represents northwest Georgia’s 11th Congressional District.

Prior to being elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2014, Congressman Loudermilk served in the Georgia General Assembly in both the State House and State Senate.

During his time in Congress, Rep. Loudermilk has served on the Committees of Science, Space and Technology, Homeland Security, the Joint Committee on the Library, Committee on House Administration, and Financial Services Committee. In his first year in Congress, he was appointed Chairman of the Oversight Subcommittee on Science, Space and Technology and served on the Homeland Security Committee’s Special Task Force for Countering Terrorism and Terrorist Travel.

In the 119th Congress, Congressman Loudermilk serves as a member of the Financial Services Committee, where he is the Vice Chair of the Subcommittee on Financial Institutions and is a member of the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations. The Subcommittee on Financial Institutions oversees banks, bank regulators, and lending, and the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations ensures that federal financial regulators are held accountable for their use of taxpayer funds. He also serves as a member of the Committee on House Administration and is a member of the Subcommittee on Elections.

Congressman Loudermilk is also a member of the conservative Republican Study Committee and is the Republican Co-Chair of the Bipartisan FinTech and Payments Caucus.

In the 118th Congress, House Speaker Kevin McCarthy tasked Congressman Loudermilk to lead the Committee on House Administration’s Subcommittee on Oversight investigation into the events at the Capitol on January 6, 2021, the security failures of the U.S. Capitol Police, and the flawed investigation of House Select Committee on January 6.

His father served as an Army medic in World War II and saw action during the D-Day invasion, Battle of the Bulge, and the Occupation of Germany. In 2019, Rep. Loudermilk was selected as one of the official delegates to represent the United States at the 75th anniversary of the D-Day invasion in Normandy.

He is native of Georgia and veteran of the U.S. Air Force. Rep. Loudermilk is a student of the Constitution and the Christian heritage of America. Barry resides in northwest Georgia with his wife, Desiree. They have three grown children, and seven grandchildren. 

https://www.barryloudermilk.com/ 
https://www.andthentheyprayed.com/

podcasts.title: Episode 111: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #111- Jody Burkeen
podcasts.description: Cody is a U.S. Navy veteran, an actor, author, husband and father. Inspired by Stallone, Cody has his eyes set on being one of the next action heroes in film. His next film, Savage, releases later this year, where he plays the former special forces soldier, Frank Savage.

podcasts.title: Episode 110: Man Up God's Way Podcast Episode #110- Clint Lowery- Sevendust
podcasts.description:
Clint Lowery, renowned guitarist and founding member of the hard rock band Sevendust, has recently experienced a profound spiritual transformation, embracing a renewed faith in Jesus Christ. Born on December 15, 1971, in Jacksonville, Florida, Lowery's musical journey has been marked by both critical acclaim and personal challenges. Despite a foundation in a Christian household—his grandfather was a Methodist preacher—Lowery's relationship with God was, for many years, distant and undefined. It wasn't until a significant health scare in 2024, involving unexplained neurological symptoms, that he fully surrendered his life to Christ. Lowery came to understand the true meaning of salvation and committed himself wholeheartedly to his faith.
This spiritual awakening has deeply influenced Lowery's personal and professional life. He completed his first tour as a "saved Christian," describing it as the most profound and spiritual experience of his career. He noted a newfound freedom from resentment and judgment, attributing this transformation to his relationship with Christ.
Lowery's journey from addiction and personal turmoil to spiritual renewal serves as a powerful testament to the transformative power of faith. His story offers hope and inspiration, illustrating how surrendering to God's grace can lead to profound personal change.

podcasts.title: Episode 109: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #109- Jenny Barber
podcasts.description:
For 15 years, Jenny has been a formidable force in the fight against sex trafficking. With a deep commitment to
supporting victims and providing advocacy, she has dedicated her career to training and empowering survivors,
advocates, and communities to combat trafficking. Overcoming her own exploitation, she launched into the anti trafficking movement with a vision to create sustainable, collaborative, and holistic approaches to abolish modern-day
slavery. She has successfully established herself as an expert in counter trafficking efforts and survivor empowerment
and continues to be a contributor to the advancement of the global anti-trafficking movement.
~10 years in both establishing and leading
Non-Profits dedicated to supporting and
advocating for trafficking survivors.
~Co-Founder of two business initiatives
providing advocacy, training, and
employment opportunities for survivors.
~Training and Education development for
businesses, educators, non-profits, and
first responders, enhancing their ability to
recognize and combat trafficking.
~Facilitated efforts among high level antitrafficking
organizations.
~Pioneered international outreach,
advocacy and training efforts for victims
and survivors of trafficking. https://talitha.com/

podcasts.title: Episode 108: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #108- Jessica Maldonado
podcasts.description:
A Texas native, Jessica has been singing professionally for over 10 years. Since the age of 3, Jessica has loved to tell stories through acting and singing. She began on the church stage, where she sang worship music and contemporary christian music. As she grew up, she began her professional career at 17 as a performer at Six Flags Fiesta Texas. Jessica became very comfortable with performing live in various venues, singing a large variety of genres such as Pop, Country, Motown, Spanish, 50's pop, Broadway show tunes, and even Rock, from more intimate stages to amphitheaters (3,000+ capacity), Jessica is no stranger to working a crowd. As more opportunities became available, she then made a turn from review shows to professional Musical Theatre at Sight and Sound Theatres. It was during this time in her career that Jessica fell in love with acting and the art of story telling. Within 3 years In her career at Sight and Sound, she made her way to the role of a lifetime "Queen Esther". She not only was able to minister to over 4,000 people daily, but the international live stream event reached to over 160 countries. "Queen Esther" went on to reach number 10 on the Top 100 Billboard Charts list in 2020 and later received a Dove nomination for "Inspirational film" in the year of 2021. After this role, Jessica went on to portray the role of Bathsheba in the Sight and Sound show "David" which received a Dove nomination for "Inspirational album" in 2022. This only solidified her passion for music and film. In 2024 Jessica made the move back to her roots in San Antonio, Texas. Since returning to Texas Jessica has begun pursuing her song writing and film career, while also leading worship in both English and Spanish throughout various local churches in the area. Jessica has begun an impressive debut into her solo music career. Her first song "At Your Feet" made a debut in the short film "The Anointing". She then went on to write a country/worship song; "Everlasting" for a documentary On Herman Mendoza's life, which premiered in New York. More than anything her passion is to encourage others through her many talents and to be a Light to everyone she encounters.
- IMDb Mini Biography By: Jessica Maldonado

podcasts.title: Episode 107: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #107- Kevin Downes- Kingdom Story Company
podcasts.description:
KEVIN DOWNES (Kingdom Story Company Co-Founder / CEO / Producer)
As co-founder and CEO of Kingdom Story Company, Kevin Downes has built an impressive career in producing, directing, acting, and writing, spanning over 30 years. As an actor, Downes played memorable roles in COURAGEOUS, MOMS’ NIGHT OUT, I CAN ONLY IMAGINE, JESUS REVOLUTION, THE BEST CHRISTMAS PAGEANT EVER, and more. 
Kingdom Story Company is the premier destination for making films that ignite a rush of hope. In partnership with Lionsgate, Kingdom Story Company prioritizes faith, inspiration, and positive impact in storytelling. Their films have consistently opened in the top ten at the box office, earning the coveted A+ CinemaScore® rating six times, including the hit film JESUS REVOLUTION in 2023.
Other notable films include AMERICAN UNDERDOG, THE JESUS MUSIC, JOHNNY CASH: THE REDEMPTION OF AN AMERICAN ICON, and I STILL BELIEVE, further solidifying the company’s reputation for high-quality storytelling. 
In 2024 Kingdom Story Company released ORDINARY ANGELS, UNSUNG HERO, WHITE BIRD, and THE BEST CHRISTMAS PAGEANT EVER nationwide. THE UNBREAKABLE BOY releases in February 2025. SARAH’S OIL and I CAN ONLY IMAGINE 2 are upcoming.

podcasts.title: Episode 106: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #106- Jody Burkeen
podcasts.description: Join us as we chat about 2024 and the current events of the day!

podcasts.title: Episode 105: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #105- Rob Decker
podcasts.description:
After years of abuse, addiction, a desperate search for praise and approval, and living in unbearable pain and isolation, Rob Decker reached a breaking point that drove him to take drastic measures. After a failed suicide attempt and a friend not giving up on him, Rob woke up alive with a new lease on life.
​​
The state of mental health issues is reaching alarming new levels, and the "silent crisis" continues to grip the minds of men and women. The problem is far too big to continue to ignore.
​
"Our world runs rampant with despair, isolation, and pain. I want to use my experiences to offer hope, healing, and restoration for others who feel lost and, in their own minds, believe that there is no hope," says Rob. "There is a 'silent crisis,' the number of people with a mental health issue is growing at an astonishing rate. It's time to bridge the gap with mental health systems and provide informal support for all who deal with various issues. It's time for men to RISE AS LIONS."
https://www.riseaslions.org/

podcasts.title: Episode 104: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #104- Brendan Schlittler
podcasts.description: Follower of Christ - Husband - Liberty Football Alum - Follow on X @_The_Good_Man

podcasts.title: Episode 103: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #103- Clint Lowery- Sevendust
podcasts.description:
Clint Lowry is an American musician, songwriter, and producer, best known as the lead guitarist and backing vocalist for the rock band Sevendust. Born on December 15, 1971, in Fayetteville, North Carolina, Lowry has been a prominent figure in the rock and metal scenes since the mid-1990s. His guitar style is characterized by heavy riffs, melodic solos, and intricate rhythms, while his vocal contributions add depth to Sevendust’s signature sound.
Musical Career:
    •    Sevendust: Lowry co-founded the band in 1994, contributing to its rise with albums like Home (1999), Animosity (2001), and Cold Day Memory (2010). He is credited with helping shape the band’s distinctive mix of metal and melody.
    •    Side Projects: During a brief hiatus from Sevendust, he formed Dark New Day, releasing the album Twelve Year Silence (2005). He has also pursued a solo career, releasing the EP Grief & Distance (2020) and the full-length album God Bless the Renegades (2020).
    •    Producer and Collaborator: Lowry has worked with other artists and bands, including writing and producing, showcasing his versatility and creative talents.
Style and Influence:
Lowry’s music blends heavy metal with alternative and post-grunge influences. His emotional lyricism and melodic sensibilities have made him a standout musician in the genre.
Outside of music, Lowry is known for his introspective personality and dedication to his craft, earning him respect from fans and peers alike.

podcasts.title: Episode 102: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #102- Vince Miller
podcasts.description: Author of 31 books and a 29-year ministry veteran, Vince Miller has served in and advised the most notable ministry names. But it hasn’t always been this way. Abandoned by his father at the age of two, Vince grew up in a challenging and anxiety-producing environment. He endured the strain of his mother’s two failed marriages, as well as her poor choices and drug use. Fortunately, during Vince’s formative teen years, his grandfather, a man of faith, stepped up to mentor Vince, and because of this, he devoted his life to mentoring others. Today, he is hosted on major video and radio platforms like RightNow Media, Faithlife TV, and Faith Radio. One of his most widely read resources is the Men's Daily Devo, read by 100,000 worldwide (beresolute.org/mdd).

podcasts.title: Episode 101: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #101- Troy Gramling
podcasts.description:
Pastor Troy Gramling, a dedicated and inspirational spiritual leader, has led Potential Church for over two decades, overseeing its remarkable growth into a vibrant congregation of over 20,000 members with a far-reaching presence spanning the United States and Latin America. His journey as a pastor is characterized by an unwavering commitment to his vision, fueled by a passion and a mission to partner with people to reach their God potential to impact the world for good.

Beyond his pastoral role, Pastor Troy Gramling is a multifaceted individual with a rich background. Before answering his divine calling, Pastor Troy honed his skills as a college basketball player and later as a coach, where he undoubtedly sharpened the values of teamwork, discipline, and dedication, which he now imparts to his congregation. His leadership insight is evident in his exceptional ability to develop and nurture leaders within the church community, ensuring that his ministry leaves a lasting legacy.

Pastor Troy is known for his innovative and unconventional teaching methods, which captivate and inspire his congregation. His creative approach to spreading the message of Jesus resonates with people from all walks of life, making the profound teachings of the gospel accessible and relevant in today’s world.

Pastor Troy, with his devoted wife, Stephanie, and their loving family, has made South Florida their home, where the family is actively involved in the ministry of Potential Church. His life’s work is a testament to the profound impact that faith can have on individuals and communities, and he continues to be a guiding light for those seeking to embrace their God-given potential and make a positive difference in the world.

podcasts.title: Episode 99: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #99- Jared Haley
podcasts.description:
About Jared Haley

Jared Haley is a follower of Jesus, pastor, and public speaker who believes God created the design for strong faith and healthy families. Serving in professional ministry for over 20 years with his Master’s Degree in Transformational Leadership and Spiritual Formation, Jared is passionate about investing in people, and helping them understand how God wired and designed them to live strong. He loves any opportunity to speak publicly, and share with others the wisdom God has given him through his years of ministry study and experience.
When not podcasting, Jared works full time for Critical Bench as a Video Production Specialist. He has created and led several other productions including The Pastors on Pop Podcast, and Practical Living: a documentary style YouTube show where Jared learned specific skills from experts, and gave them the opportunity to share their individual stories. He continues to be active in the local Church serving as a leader, vocalist on the worship team, and volunteering to help his wife who oversees the child care ministry for all church services.
Although originally from Denver Colorado, Jared currently resides in Palm Harbor, FL with his wife of 15 years, Cairistiona, and their four children. He is an avid Denver sports fan, and is always ready for a fun game night, or watching a good movie. It is always a blessing when he can get outdoors to play soccer, softball, golf, or take a trip to the beach.

podcasts.title: Episode 97: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #97- Dr Jason Lee McKinney
podcasts.description:
Multi-award-winning singer/songwriter/recording artist and college professor, Dr. Jason Lee McKinney, has added "author" to his list of accomplishments with the unveiling of his debut book, Deconstructing a Disciple’s Doubt, recently released to retail from WordCrafts Press.


Perhaps better known as the founder and frontman for the acclaimed Americana roots band that shares his name, McKinney has spent more than a decade cementing a reputation for himself and The Jason Lee McKinney Band in the competitive world of modern-day roots music, resulting in worldwide recognition, national and international tours, and a number of industry awards, including a 2021 Josie Music Award for R&B/Soul Entertainer of the Year, a 2019 Josie Music Award for R&B/Soul Vocalist of the Year, and a 2014 Independent Country Music Association Award for Artist of the Year.


McKinney’s independent artistic creativity drove him to pursue multiple academic degrees, including a BA in Management, an MBA, an MA in Philosophy and Apologetics, and an Ed.D. in Leadership and Professional Practice.

podcasts.title: Episode 91: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #91- Joshua Broome
podcasts.description:
A former porn star-turned-preacher reveals seven lies our culture tells us and guides us to the only thing that creates authentic happiness: a relationship with Jesus.

Joshua Broome knows this firsthand. He had a successful career in the adult film industry, making lots of money and achieving notoriety and all the markers of success. And yet he felt empty inside. After hitting rock bottom, he cried out to Jesus ... and it was only then that he was fully redeemed.

In 7 Lies That Will Ruin Your Life, Joshua examines seven things we are told will make us happy and shows how each of them points us back to our need for God. 

Using his own journey as a point of reference, 7 Lies That Will Ruin Your Life pairs his personal experiences with Biblical truths, demonstrating both how we can be deceived and the healing truth that comes when we realize that Jesus really is enough.

https://www.joshuabroome.me/about-joshua-broome

podcasts.description: Kevin Downes is a California native. He grew up in Visalia, just 3 hours north of Los Angeles where he shot his early films with his brother, Bobby and friend, David A.R. White He met Jon Erwin, while performing in the movie, Courageous, and the two partnered up along with Andy Erwin to create Moms' Night Out. They followed immediately with the film, Woodlawn and while in post production, presented the Erwin Brothers with the story behind, I Can Only Imagine, which became their 3rd feature film together. Soon after, they formed Kingdom and signed a first look deal with Lionsgate for feature films and television series to be released under the new banner. Kevin lives in Orange County, California with his wife Catherine and 3 sons, Benicio, Nathaniel and Joshua.

podcasts.description:

For more information about Kingdom Story Company,

podcasts.title: Episode 88: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #88- Jon Gunn- Ordinary Angels
podcasts.description:
JON GUNN BIO
Since graduating with a film degree from Ithaca College in 1995, Jon Gunn has worked as a writer, director, editor, and producer. His work ranges from independent comedy and drama to studio tentpoles. 


As a director, his feature films include MERCY STREETS, MY DATE WITH DREW, LIKE DANDELION DUST, and THE CASE FOR CHRIST, and have grossed more than $40 million at the box office. MY DATE WITH DREW, which had a worldwide theatrical release in the summer of 2005, swept the festival circuit for Best Feature.


LIKE DANDELION DUST is a Fox feature drama starring Mira Sorvino and Barry Pepper, which Gunn co-wrote with Oscar-nominated screenwriter Stephen J. Rivele. The film won more than 30 festival awards and was released theatrically in the fall of 2010. THE CASE FOR CHRIST stars Mike Vogel, Erika Christensen, Faye Dunaway, and Robert Forster and grossed $17 million in theaters. 


Gunn has also co-written numerous studio movies, including the comedy IAN THE VAMPIRE for Fox Searchlight, THE MAGIC 8 BALL for Paramount, THE NUTCRACKER for Universal, IMAGINARY FRIENDS for Skydance, MONOPOLY for Hasbro/Sony, and multiple features for DreamWorks animation, including ALCATRAZ VERSUS THE EVIL LIBRARIANS and B.O.O. THE BUREAU OF OTHERWORLDLY OPERATIONS, starring Seth Rogen, Melissa McCarthy and Bill Murray. 


Prior to JESUS REVOLUTION, Gunn co-wrote the musical biopic I STILL BELIEVE with Jon Erwin, based on the life of singer Jeremy Camp, which was released by Lionsgate in 2020.

podcasts.title: Episode 87: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #87- "Fastest Pastor" Don Wickstrum
podcasts.description:
Don’s Bio,

 From the time he was born, the odds seemed stacked against Don Wickstrum. He was always fighting. Always scratching and clawing. He was the perpetual underdog. That took on new meaning when he got the worst news possible: cancer.

 At that moment, he was forced to confront the question, “What would you do if you had a year to live?” How Don answered that question led to a whole new life – a life he only dreamed of since a young age. Today, he’s telling that story all around the world in a captivating way, both in person and through the dramatic documentary of his life called “Chasing Hope.”

 From the age of eight, Don had a vivid dream: to make it to the top of one of the most well-known mountains in the continental United States while going as fast as he could in a  race car. He never envisioned the nightmare of a cancer diagnosis would make his childhood dream a reality. 

 In 2018, Don heard the words that so many people dread: “You have cancer.” As someone who had struggled with anxiety and depression in the past, his colon cancer diagnosis and especially the subsequent fight took its toll. But as he and his family prayed and created a game plan for treatment, something else started emerging: resolve. 

 As he stared down the real possibility of death, Don remembered something that had always been on his bucket list: his childhood dream of racing to the top of Pike’s Peak, one of Colorado's famed “14ers.” Drawing on his newfound resolve as well as his strong faith in Jesus, he decided to pursue the Pike’s Peak International Hill Climb, considered one of the deadliest races in America. It took everything he had just to attempt it – let alone complete it.

 After several failed attempts, in 2022 he tamed the full mountain – with its 156 turns over a 12.42-mile course – all while battling cancer. His spiritual convictions and racing prowess earned him the nickname, “Fastest Pastor.” That’s ironic, considering Don spent much of his young life identifying as an atheist, which gave way to his belief in Jesus after he fully pursued the evidence.

 His life story, as well as his racing legacy, was captured in the 2022 I Am Second film called “Chasing Hope.”

 Before Wickstrum – born in Illinois and raised in Wisconsin – became a racing legend, though, he was a successful entrepreneur, building and leading one of the biggest robotics integration companies in the country. That journey started when he got an engineering internship in college, which he admits he had no business getting. He sold his booming business after his cancer diagnosis to pursue his dream of racing and reaching people with a message of hope. 

 Today, Don is passionate about bringing that message to whoever will listen as he travels the country speaking. He draws on his life experience to show that overcoming your past is possible through the help of Jesus Christ. Those experiences include being told by his doctors he would not make it after birth, surviving multiple sexual assault attempts, dyslexia, the death of a close family member, mental health struggles, building a multi-million dollar business, creating a ministry, being a family man, his own failures, and even family betrayal. 

 Don resides in Monroe, WI, with his wife, Mary, and their five children. While not racing and speaking, he serves as the associate pastor of Grace Church in New Glarus, WI. 

 You can read more about Don’s current work at fastestpastor.com.

podcasts.title: Episode 86: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #86- Mike Coy
podcasts.description:
Mike lives in Colorado Springs, CO as a Senior Benefits Consultant for Aflac and celebrated his 45th year with Aflac on November 1, 2021. He is recognized as one of the leading healthcare gurus in the nation and is a much sought after keynote speaker, with topics ranging from Aflac’s solutions to Heath Care Reform and Employee Benefits to personal stories about succeeding in business, in sports, and in life. He firmly believes that not including Aflac in your employee choices of health care is a huge mistake in today’s very confusing health care marketplace and will gladly explain WHY.
Mike is an honest man who puts his money where his mouth is, personally donating funds to the Aflac Children’s Cancer Hospital throughout his tenure with the organization. Since 1995, Aflac has raised and donated more than $110 million to fund cancer research. Much of Aflac’s support comes from its independent sales agents, such as Mike, who contribute donations from their monthly commission checks, as well as Aflac employees who contribute each month through payroll deductions. And it is making a difference. Leukemia is the biggest killer of children. Fifteen years ago the survival rate was a mere 36%, today the survival rate is 80%. But no one will be satisfied until there is a 100% survival rate for these young kids.
Mike’s inspiration comes from the Jerry Lewis telethons, which raised billions of dollars to fund research for a cure to Muscular Dystrophy. He used his celebrity to help others, even though he didn’t have to. Mike may not be a celebrity, except in his own mind, but he has been through cancer, survived it, and wants to give back to find a cure. He is providing multiple ways for you to join him on his journey of raising funds for cancer research… buy his inspirational books and Aflac branded products, join him at entertaining live events, and hire him to speak to your organization.
Mike’s Motivational Books
Mike’s first book, “Incredible Business” was a labor of love and a great co-author collaboration on how building relationships and gaining referrals beats cold-calling all day long.
His second book, “I Chose…Live” recounts his battle with Cancer and how he is winning the war. He raises awareness for Cancer prevention and early detection; discusses modern advances in Cancer research and treatment; and reminds the reader to keep smiling no matter how much “life’s challenges” come their way.
In his newest book, “A Random Act of Kindness”, Mike speaks to how simple gestures might help change a life plus it just might save a life.

podcasts.title: Episode 85: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #85- Brent Pohlman- CEO Midwest Labs
podcasts.description:
Brent Pohlman, owner and CEO of Midwest Laboratories, strives to bring his A-game to work daily. At 15, Brent landed his first job at Midwest Laboratories, the company his father founded in 1975. He became CEO in 2016 and is passionate about teaching others to lead effectively. As a person of faith, he coaches people up, leads from the heart, and has achieved success by focusing on people, processes, and technology—in that order.

Grateful to build on his father’s legacy, Brent aspired to be a leader and owner of a great company. His transformation has convinced him that leaders must look deeper within themselves to find their true identities. He believes the only way to lead is from the heart and is passionate about helping others learn how to lead others effectively.

Brent has been a guest on the weekly radio show Grow Omaha and the business podcast Momentum for the Entrepreneurial Personality Type. He earned a BA in business administration and a BS in secondary education from Concordia University, then his MBA in human resources from the University of Nebraska.

Working out and reading are among Brent’s hobbies. He also enjoys attending Kansas City Chiefs games and live concerts by artists from the 1970s, 80s, and 90s. Brent and his family live in Omaha, Nebraska.

podcasts.title: Episode 84: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #84- Colonel Richard Mendelow
podcasts.description:
Colonel Rich “Bucky” Mendelow was born in Johannesburg, South Africa. His family immigrated to the U.S. in 1976. He attended James E. Taylor High School in Katy, Texas and entered the Naval Reserve Officer Training Corps at Duke University in 1984. He was commissioned as a Second Lieutenant in 1988 and following infantry training at The Basic School, in Quantico, Virginia, he received orders to Pensacola, Florida for Flight School. He received his Naval Aviator’s wings in March 1991. He flew the EA-6B Prowler and after numerous deployments, left active duty in 1997 to pursue a career as an airline pilot.

In 2008, Major Mendelow returned to the Marine Corps Reserves. Later that year, he reported to Camp Lejeune, NC for deployment to Iraq with the Second Marine Expeditionary Force. In June 2010, Major Mendelow reported to Camp Pendleton, CA for deployment to Afghanistan as a Border Advisor Team Leader with the First Marine Expeditionary Force. While deployed to Afghanistan, he completed over 200 patrols in 210 days. In 2013, he deployed to Afghanistan again, this time as the Executive Officer of a combat assault support squadron. Lieutenant Colonel Mendelow flew 20 combat missions and over 80 combat flight hours. He was promoted to Colonel in 2017 and spent the last five years of his career working on Marine Corps’ cultural initiatives. He retired from the Reserves in 2022 after a career which spanned five decades. His personal decorations include the Bronze Star, Air Medal, Meritorious Service Medal, Navy & Marine Corps Commendation Medal, Navy & Marine Corps Achievement Medal, and Combat Action Ribbon.
Get his book here https://courageouschristianity.today/read/

podcasts.title: Episode 83: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #83- The Squad- Jody and Fragoza
podcasts.description: Join the Squad as we chat about anything and everything!

podcasts.title: Episode 82: Man Up Monday Podcast Episode #82- Bryan Rucker
podcasts.description:
Gangs, drugs and dead-end jobs. The cards of life were stacked against Bryan Rucker.

But a new opportunity presented a chance for Rucker to change. While working for the duck-call empire, he observed something unique about the Robertson family. He felt acceptance. He felt love. Moreover, he discovered hope that surpassed understanding. He began to build community within the dynasty.

Finally, Rucker felt like things were starting to go his way – but old habits die hard, and he ended up in trouble again. Would he take advantage of this seemingly last chance to change?
